data.title: Brushing Basics That Actually Stick
data.excerpt: Simple habits that protect enamel and gums—without turning your routine into a chore.
data.coverImageAlt: Toothbrush and timer on a clean bathroom counter
body: Consistency beats intensity. Two minutes, twice a day, with a soft brush and fluoride toothpaste is the baseline most people need.
Angle the bristles toward the gumline and use gentle pressure—scrubbing harder doesn’t clean better; it can wear enamel and irritate gums.
If you’re rebuilding a habit, pair brushing with something you already do daily (like brewing coffee or starting your evening wind-down).

seo.title: Brushing Basics | Amity Gentle Dental Blog
seo.description: Practical brushing tips from Amity Gentle Dental.

data.title: When Dental Anxiety Shows Up
data.excerpt: What helps before you even sit in the chair—and how we support nervous patients.
data.coverImageAlt: Calm waiting area with soft lighting
body: Anxiety is common, and it’s not something you should have to “just get over.” Small signals—like noise, past experiences, or uncertainty—can make visits feel bigger than they are.
We slow down, explain what to expect, and build breaks into longer appointments when needed. If Solea Sleep or other comfort options are appropriate, we’ll discuss them clearly.
If you have specific triggers, tell us when you book. That helps us prepare the right environment from the first minute.

seo.title: Dental Anxiety Tips | Amity Gentle Dental Blog
seo.description: Supportive strategies for anxious dental patients.

data.title: First Visits for Kids: What to Expect
data.excerpt: A warm introduction to the dental home—focused on trust, not perfection.
body: Early visits are as much about comfort as cleaning. We want kids to learn that dental care is a normal, friendly part of life.
Parents play a huge role: positive language and calm energy go a long way. We’ll guide you on age-appropriate hygiene at home, too.
If your child is nervous, we’ll adjust pacing. Small wins build confidence for the next visit.

seo.title: Kids’ First Dental Visits | Amity Gentle Dental Blog
seo.description: What families can expect during pediatric dental introductions.
